paper_title: Model and experiment design for middle Miocene climate simulations

methods.description: Configuration of the FOAM version 1.5 climate model for simulating middle Miocene climate conditions, including atmospheric and oceanic components, spatial resolution, and coupling mechanisms.

steps.description: FOAM version 1.5 is a fully coupled OAGCM with a parallelised version of Community Climate Model 2 (CCM2) and upgraded atmospheric physics following CCM3.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure FOAMv1.5 with CCM2 and CCM3 physics for climate modeling

steps.description: The soil hydrology module is replaced by a simple bucket model (0.15 m deep). Overflow is routed to the ocean using a parallel river transport.
steps.searchable_summary: Implement simple bucket model for soil hydrology and route overflow to ocean

steps.description: The atmospheric component runs at R15 (4.5° × 7.5°) spatial resolution, with 18 vertical layers.
steps.searchable_summary: Set atmospheric component resolution to R15 with 18 vertical layers

steps.description: The oceanic component is a parallel ocean model similar to the Geophysical Fluid Dynamics Laboratory's Modular Ocean Model (MOM) with a 128 × 128 Mercator grid (1.4° × 2.8°) and 24 vertical levels.
steps.searchable_summary: Configure oceanic component with Mercator grid and 24 vertical levels

steps.description: The atmospheric and oceanic components are linked by a coupler, which calculates and interpolates fluxes between the two components.
steps.searchable_summary: Link atmospheric and oceanic components with a coupler for flux calculation

methods.description: Setup of simulations for the middle Miocene period using specific palaeogeography, vegetation, and CO2 concentration settings to test the impact of the Tethys seaway closure on oceanic circulation and climate.

steps.description: Simulations are forced with a palaeogeography derived from Herold et al. (2008), with differences such as lower reliefs, an open Central American Isthmus, a closed Bering Strait, and a southward shift of Australia.
steps.searchable_summary: Set palaeogeography for middle Miocene simulations based on Herold et al. (2008)

steps.description: Four sensitivity experiments are performed with various gateway depths to test the impact of the Tethys seaway closure.
steps.searchable_summary: Conduct sensitivity experiments with different Tethys seaway depths

steps.description: Miocene vegetation is based on Wolfe (1985) with changes such as the absence of desert and larger expansion of tropical and subtropical forests.
steps.searchable_summary: Set Miocene vegetation based on Wolfe (1985) for simulations

steps.description: Experiments are integrated for 3000 years without flux correction, using the average of the last 30 years for analysis.
steps.searchable_summary: Integrate experiments for 3000 years and analyze last 30 years
